frameworks.name: resource-rational choice
frameworks.one_line: choice optimizes precision-cost vs benefit trade-off rather than logical consistency

frameworks.name: bounded rationality
frameworks.one_line: extends bounded rationality with explicit cost-benefit optimization of precision

frameworks.name: rational analysis
frameworks.one_line: evaluates whether observed irrationalities are adaptive under constraints

core_question: Are the apparent irrationalities of human choice an inefficient patchwork, or an optimal solution to a precision-cost trade-off?
key_assumptions: increasing choice-mechanism precision incurs a cost
marginal benefits of precision decline with increasing precision
biological choosers face a precision-cost vs benefit trade-off
observed choice behavior reflects an optimization, not a malfunction
manipulations: interdisciplinary synthesis review — no experimental manipulation
key_findings: logically inconsistent choices are consistent across disciplines and decades
many puzzling inconsistencies fit a single precision-cost optimization account
a rationally imprecise strategy emerges under cost-benefit constraints
interpretations: human choice is efficiently irrational — optimal for efficiency, not rationality
inconsistencies are adaptive consequences of biological optimization, not failures
limitations_noted: synthesis-level argument rather than a new empirical test
